TextENGNew: Good morning~! Great job yesterday, Kei-chan!
You too, Mion! Yesterday was a blast!
Oh, right—Keiichi-kun, thank you so much! For Mr. Bear!
Glad you like it. Don't use it for a pin cushion, though.
I-I would never do that...!
Still chatting happily, we stepped into the classroom—and there, waiting for us, was a mop bucket filled to the brim with water.
My foot plunged straight into it.
My, how diligent of you—scrubbing the floor first thing in the morning~!

*stomp stomp stomp*
*fwip*
*thwap*
Ah—waaaahhh!! Keiichi-san flicked my forehead~!!
You should set a more devious trap next time.
Rena convulsed in ecstasy at the sight; Mion watched her with weary eyes.
Everything was exactly the same as always.
Not a classmate missing, not a thing out of place.
The vague unease that had lingered in the back of my mind since last night dissolved all at once.
What's wrong, Keiichi-kun? Maybe you didn't get enough sleep? Maybe?
Slept like the dead. I could do an encore during class if you want.
You can't do that...!
Mion alerted us that the teacher was coming and everyone filed back to their seats.
Good morning, everyone. Yesterday was the festival. I'd like to thank those of you who stayed until the very end to help clean up.
We were thoroughly unhelpful, having done none of the cleaning.
If anything, we'd just run amok through the festival.
changeReason: Old translation contains extra content not present in the Japanese source.

TextENGNew: Now then. This time of year, there may be reporters from magazines and such visiting from other places to cover the festival.
I'd been about to tune it out as the usual lecture, but something in her tone changed and pulled me back.
If any of you are asked for interviews, please don't say anything vague or irresponsible. Is that understood?
Yes, ma'am! everyone answered in unison.
She'd been deliberately oblique, but I understood what she was trying to say.
After all, this was now the fifth year.
Reporters from cheap tabloids would be creeping around, hoping the curse had claimed another victim.
All it ever did was deepen Hinamizawa's bad reputation.
She was telling us not to say anything thoughtless just because we thought it was funny.
I was now among those who had to stay quiet.
I found it quietly touching that I felt a sense of solidarity over something so small.
After school, as expected, the club convened.
Given that everyone was tired from yesterday, today would be a relaxed, quiet—yeah, right!
Today, let's go with a classic deduction game! What do you think of this one?
Oh! A game from overseas! Looks interesting!
The instructions are in English, by the way. But the rules aren't too complicated!
...I won't lose, you hear.
I happen to be not bad at this game.
Rena seemed to be out of her depth here.
The rules: whoever correctly identifies the murderer, the weapon, and the crime scene wins!
There were apparently three kinds of cards: suspects, weapons, and locations.
Looking at the suspect cards—they had names on them. Mion, Rika... everyone. Oh, there's even a card with my name on it!
There are all sorts of weapons too! Axe, knife, poison! And the locations are great! Living room, study, courtyard!
So—one card is removed from each category. Those are the correct answer cards.
The remaining cards are shuffled and divided among everyone. Then we question each other to figure out which cards were removed.
I see. Whoever holds no card for a category—that's the murderer!
When you think you know the answer, raise your hand! The game stops and we check whether you're right.
A correct answer earns you a point! A wrong answer costs you a point! Either way the game ends, so all cards are collected and we start again!
You'll understand once you play! It really makes you think!
You'll get confused if you don't take notes.
I'll pick up the rest as I go. So, Mion! What's the penalty this time?
Everyone's eyes turned to Mion!
Let's see... We're all still a bit tired from yesterday, so let's go easy today. How about the 'errand duty' penalty? You go buy things that everyone asks you to!
So basically—going out to buy juice and snacks and things.
...Today's penalty feels surprisingly mild.
Does it, really...?
So Keiichi-san thinks the same as I do?
Yeah... you never know what you'll end up being sent to buy.
Knowing Mion, the loser would surely be dispatched to buy hemorrhoid cream or some suspicious energy drink!
The cards were promptly shuffled and dealt to each player.
Since the murderer card, the correct weapon, and the crime scene have all been removed, every card in my hand is 'innocent.'
I'll go first since I'm club president! ...'Mion,' 'Knife,' 'Study'!
You choose one suspect, one weapon, and one crime scene and ask all the other players.
If you hold any one of those cards, you must say 'I have one'—though you don't have to say which one. That's the key.
Oh, I've got one.
I do too.
...I have one as well.
My, that's a clean sweep.
Three players answered when asked about 'Mion,' 'Knife,' and 'Study.'
So that means 'Mion' and 'Knife' and 'Study' are all innocent. Better write that down...
Then I'll go... 'Rena,' 'Axe,' 'Lounge'!
Hm, only two people said they had a match? That means one of 'Rena,' 'Axe,' or 'Lounge' is part of the crime!?
No, wait. Even if they say they have one, they don't have to say how many!
changeReason: Character name 'Satoko' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: It's possible that either Satoko or Rika-chan has two of the cards...!!
changeReason: Character name 'Satoko' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: Then it's Kei-chan's turn. Go ahead~!
W-Wait a moment...! I'm still sorting things out!
...This game requires more thought than I expected...!
This is bad... I have a feeling it's going to be rough!!
Got it! I've identified the murderer!!
What!? Already!?
Still spinning in circles, I watched as Mion raised her hand.
The murderer is 'Rika-chan'! The weapon is 'Poison,' and the crime scene is the 'Infirmary'! Well!?
The answer cards were retrieved from the box to verify Mion's deduction...
Correct!!
Argh~! I was one move away~! I couldn't tell whether it was 'Poison' or 'Pistol'~!!
I'd never use a pistol. Slow poisoning is far preferable.
Rika-chan delivered that terrifying sentiment with the calmest little face.
Everyone was in an uproar about how close they'd been. Rena and I were the odd ones out.
...How did you do, Keiichi-kun...? Rena couldn't figure it out at all...
changeReason: Character name 'Rena' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: Don't worry. I hadn't a clue either.
Why are you both so hopeless! At this rate you'll end up in last place together~
That would be terrible!!
The two of us off buying things together as a pair—and if one of those things turned out to be some kind of family planning product...!
...Keiichi's expression says he's not very good at this game.
What! That's absolutely not true!! I'll show you Sherlock Maebara's true abilities next round!
My, my~! That sounds just wonderful!!! I shall crush you utterly!
After that, I managed to get a rough feel for the game—but I was still two or three steps behind everyone else.
Mion seemed to be extracting information even from the way others phrased their questions. The difference in experience was overwhelming!
The only way to overcome this disadvantage was to resort to ruthlessness!!!
I abandoned the narrow thinking of 'deduction' and switched to club-game survival mode...
The important thing isn't finding the murderer—it's winning the game...!!
Eureka!!!
Ah, sorry—just need to use the bathroom for a sec.
Take your sweet time with your little business~!
Mii-chan, that's vulgar...!
I stepped out into the hallway with the game on hold.
The air was crisp out here.
A reminder of just how stale the classroom had become with all our body heat.
On the school grounds, some of our classmates were still playing around and making noise.
After spending a little time cooling off my overheated head, Rena came walking toward me.
...Keiichi-kun, did you call for me?
Yeah, I did.
As I stood up, I'd given Rena a quick meaningful glance.
Glad it got through.
Let's not waste time—straight to the point. Rena, what's your record at this game?
She was momentarily taken aback, then hesitantly opened her mouth.
Um... well... honestly. I've lost every single time. ...Why do you ask, I wonder. I wonder?
I'd already guessed as much from how tentatively she'd been playing.
At this rate I'll end up the same way. I can't figure out a way to pull off a comeback in this game!
Th-Then... both of us will be taking the penalty together...?
Hey now! Don't accept defeat already! Let's both win this—together!
B-But... how!?

I brought my lips close to Rena's ear.
*whisper whisper whisper*
Huh? I-Is that even allowed!?
Have you forgotten, Rena? The second rule of our club! Do everything in your power to obtain victory!
Y-Yeah! I'll—I'll give it my best.
Our plan was a perfectly standard one.
At the start of each round, after everyone received their cards, there'd be a brief moment when all eyes were down—taking notes, reviewing hands.
In that moment, Rena and I, sitting side by side, would show each other our cards!
changeReason: Character name 'Rena' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: A quick flash under the desk—nobody would catch it!
We'd begin each round with twice the information everyone else had!
More information means fewer wasted questions!!
With this plan, we start with a clear lead over the others!
Y-Yeah! With this, maybe we can actually win...!
We traded a grin.
If I was the corrupt merchant, then Rena was the crooked magistrate. Heh heh, pleasure doing business!
Let's go back right now! We'll work as a pair and surprise everyone!
Hey! If we go back together there's no point! It'd be obvious we cooked something up!
Oh, sorry. Then what should we do...?
You go back first, Rena. I'll head back after washing my face or something.
Okay. Got it!
Rena turned around and headed back to the classroom.
Hmm, maybe I should have held her a bit longer before sending her back.
Going back too soon would look suspicious. If Mion caught on, things could get awkward...
Well, they'd figure it out sooner or later. But by then, Rena and I would be on a winning streak!
We'd have racked up enough points to avoid last place by the time Mion realized what was happening!!
changeReason: Character name 'Rena' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: Excellent!! Not bad at all!!
Maebara-kun. Do you have a moment?
Huh? It's the teacher. ...Of all the timing!
"What is it? I'm in the middle of something important..."
changeReason: Old translation appears significantly truncated compared to new translation and Japanese source.

TextENGNew: You have a visitor, Maebara-kun. Please make your way to the entrance.
changeReason: Character name 'Maebara' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: A visitor? It's not like I run a hotel.
They've been waiting. Please hurry.
The timing was terrible—right before my decisive moment. But a visitor asking specifically for me was strange enough to make me curious.
I didn't want to waste time, so I decided to take care of it quickly.
The entryway was a stark contrast of burning sunlight and deep shadow.
Waiting there, sweating in the heat, was a middle-aged man with his jacket slung under one arm and his tie loosened to an embarrassing degree.
Are you Maebara-san? Keiichi Maebara-san?
He was obviously not a local, and we had obviously never met.
changeReason: Old translation contains substantially more content than new translation.

TextENGNew: Ever since Tomitake-san, I seemed to keep running into middle-aged men.
That's right. And who are you?
My car's air conditioning is on—let's talk there. It's rather hot out here, isn't it?
Casually ignoring my question, he pointed at a car parked by the school gate and started walking.
You've got to be kidding. I had no intention of going anywhere with some stranger I'd never met—and he was asking me to get in his car?
I don't bite. Go on, come in.
He had the back door open and was waving me over.
I didn't like this man—but I was also curious what he had to say.
Even knowing that conversations that start like this never lead anywhere good.
The car was genuinely cool.
A car with air conditioning was supposed to be a luxury item.
My father's car certainly didn't have one.
Let me know if it's too cold for you, all right? I tend to crank it up rather high.
So what do you want with me?
Two could play at the ignoring game.
changeReason: Old translation contains substantially more content than new translation.

TextENGNew: He pulled a small notebook from his breast pocket, flipped through it, and removed a photograph that was tucked inside.
It showed the face of a man with a rather vacant, sleepy expression.
If you know anything about this individual, please tell me.
Who is this?
The picture was as expressionless as a driver's license photo—his own close friends might not recognize him.
There were scribbles on his shirt—in permanent marker. Starting with yours, Maebara-san, and bearing the signatures of several of your classmates.
...Huh... this... this is Tomitake-san!?
The photograph held none of his usual slightly unreliable, quietly cheerful air.
Just a blank, sleepy face...
And do you know this woman?
I had a feeling I knew before I even looked.
...I don't know her name, but she was the woman who was with Tomitake-san last night.
I didn't know her name, but I knew she was a resident of Hinamizawa.
When was the last time you saw these two?
The night of the Watanagashi. We spoke together. They seemed quite close.
Did anything seem off to you? Anything at all. Please, speak freely.
Being pressed for every last detail was honestly very uncomfortable.
By now, I had a fairly good idea of who this man was.
Did something happen to them?
No answer.
So I returned the silence in kind.
He was a cop. I was almost certain of it—no, completely certain.
So then why?
Why was he asking about Tomitake-san? What had happened to him?
And more importantly—why me?
There had to be plenty of people who knew far more about him than I did.
The drone of the air conditioner felt unbearably loud.
After a long, heavy silence, he finally spoke.
You only moved here recently, didn't you, Maebara-san? Are you aware of the... stories... about Oyashiro-sama?
My heart lurched.
I was terrible at hiding things. I probably showed it all over my face.
Not at all? If you don't know, that's perfectly fine...
...Well... I've at least heard of it. Tomitake-san told me...
How much do you know?
The dismemberment. The accidental death. The sudden illness and the suicide. The housewife beaten to death.
Strange deaths occurring every year, always on the night of the Watanagashi...
I didn't think Tomitake-san would have held anything back. That should be everything...
No—I didn't want to think there was anything more than that.
Maebara-san... do you believe in the curse? Speak candidly.
I don't.
Immediate.
It wasn't so much that I didn't believe—it was more that I was on the verge of believing, and I wanted to drive that thought away. That feeling was stronger.
Really? Then I'm glad to hear it. You really are city-raised, aren't you.
What does believing or not believing have to do with anything? My friends are waiting, so could you get to the point?
The man in this photograph died last night.
My mind went blank.
...What? What happened to Tomitake-san?
Of all the nights—he died last night. The night of the Watanagashi. I imagine you understand what that implies, Maebara-san.
Implies... I can't think of any...
It wasn't about how he died or why.
The problem was that he died on the night of the Watanagashi.
Which meant... this year too... Oyashiro-sama's curse had...!
The death of Jirou Tomitake is still confidential. You can understand why, can't you?
I didn't want to understand.
...Please, tell me. What happened?
...It was unusual. Perhaps a bit too graphic for someone raised here in Hinamizawa.
He was being deliberately roundabout, but it made me hesitate for a moment about whether I actually wanted to hear the rest.
I'd already learned that forcing myself to know things I shouldn't had only ever led to regret.
And this had that same feeling about it...
changeReason: Old translation contains extra content not present in the Japanese source.

TextENGNew: One of our patrol wagons discovered him while returning from festival security detail. The time was five minutes before midnight.
The location... let me think... you know where the road into town transitions to pavement? Down at the base of the slope. He was on the shoulder there.
That stretch of road had almost no streetlights.
Nothing but car headlights and moonlight in the dark.
It was pure chance that anyone spotted Tomitake-san collapsed on the shoulder.
Tomitake-san, face-down in a pool of blood. Blood spread across the asphalt.
At first we assumed it was a hit-and-run. But the moment the officer approached to check for consciousness, he noticed something was wrong.
...His throat had been torn open.
Fr-From a knife or something...!?
No. Fingernails.
Fingernails!? Fingernails as in—these fingernails, the ones on your hands!? He clawed his own throat apart with those!?
The coroner's examination revealed that the nails were his own.
Wh—what does that mean...?
In other words—this wasn't murder. It was suicide.
Tomitake-san, for whatever reason, had begun clawing at his own throat—as hard as he could—with his fingernails!
Tearing the skin until the blood ran... and still not stopping. Still clawing!!
changeReason: Character name 'Tomitake' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: With a force that could peel away the nails themselves—still clawing! Still clawing!!
Until at last the nails reached the vital vessels that must never be damaged—
—and blood sprayed all around him.
Coughing blood. Convulsing. Collapsing. And finally—still.
We considered the possibility of drugs, but nothing of that nature was detected.
But—this was suicide!?
I had never heard of anything like this!!
A death this utterly abnormal...!!!
If this wasn't a 'mysterious death,' what was...?
In all five years of incidents—this one resembled a curse most of all...!
As if to demonstrate, to both Tomitake-san who had insisted there was no curse, and to me—that we had been wrong!
There are a few more suspicious elements. From secretions, perspiration, hair loss, and other physical signs, we believe Tomitake-san was in a state of extreme agitation immediately before his death.
changeReason: Character name 'Tomitake' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: Well, naturally... It's hard to imagine someone tearing their own throat apart while calm.

Wounds on his hands matched a piece of lumber found discarded nearby. There are marks on the surrounding trees and guardrails where he struck them. Blood traces scattered all around... Meaning, in short...
changeReason: Character name 'Tomitake' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: While tearing open his own throat, covered in blood, Tomitake-san was simultaneously swinging a piece of lumber.
changeReason: Character name 'Tomitake' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: There were external injuries on the body that were not self-inflicted. There is a high probability Tomitake-san was assaulted. Based on the nature of the wounds, multiple attackers are possible.
To summarize:
Tomitake-san was surrounded and attacked.
He fled in a state of panic through the night roads. Cornered, he grabbed a piece of lumber and tried to fight back.
All the while, in his delirium, he had begun clawing at his own throat. Still clawing!!
And then—he was gone.
The estimated time of death is between nine and eleven in the evening. In other words—it happened almost immediately after you spoke with Tomitake-san at the festival and parted ways.
We'd all signed Tomitake-san's shirt together... and said goodbye... Right after that...
Wait—Tomitake-san was with that woman. What about her?
changeReason: Character name 'Tomitake' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: She's missing. She hasn't reported to work, and she didn't return home last night. There is a very high probability she was involved in the incident.
For a moment, I could only sit there, hollowed out.
I had never imagined that something happening to someone close to me could strike this deep...
The time I had shared with Tomitake-san was absurdly brief, in retrospect.
But we'd attended the same festival, spent the same evening together, competed in the same game. He was one of us.
We've been pursuing the investigation from every angle, but when it comes to Oyashiro-sama's curse, the locals just won't talk...
I understood that well.
I myself had known nothing until Tomitake-san—someone from outside—told me.
...That's why you came to me? Because I'm not originally from Hinamizawa?
If that was his reason for coming to me, I should have been furious. But somehow I wasn't.
If things continue as they have, the conclusion will inevitably be that Jirou Tomitake died from Oyashiro-sama's curse.
The man turned away from me and gazed into the distance.
On the night of the Watanagashi, during a sacred ritual, he was carelessly snapping photographs—so he incurred Oyashiro-sama's wrath. That's how it will be written.
There's no way Oyashiro-sama's curse would have targeted Tomitake-san...!
Tomitake-san was killed by human hands—and cowardly ones at that.
I was not going to let it be swept under the rug as the work of some curse!
I agree entirely. There is no such thing as divine punishment or curses!!
The tension broke as his expression softened into a warm smile.
That is precisely the situation, Maebara-san. Cooperation from those in Hinamizawa who don't believe in the curse is indispensable. You understand?
Neither Tomitake-san nor I believed in curses.
But at this rate, Tomitake-san's death would simply be tallied as the fifth-year incident.
That would mean Hinamizawa had rejected him. That was the one thing I couldn't allow.
changeReason: Character name 'Tomitake' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: Tomitake-san was one of us.
He didn't live in Hinamizawa, but he came every year—in some ways, he was more of a Hinamizawa person than I was.
There was absolutely no way Oyashiro-sama's curse would have fallen on someone like him...!
changeReason: Character name 'Tomitake' present in old translation but absent in new.

TextENGNew: But there's nothing I can actually do to help. I have no idea what happened that night...
Oh, no—it's enough if you simply let me know if you see or hear anything that strikes you as odd.
If I see or hear. He was speaking in the future tense.
Things, people, rumors—anything will do. It doesn't need to be certain. Here's my phone number. If I'm not in, just leave a message with whoever picks up.
He handed me a slip of paper with his number on it. I hesitated for a moment before taking it.
If I took this—whether I wanted to or not, I'd be pulled into this.
Please help me see to it that Tomitake-san's death is not left unanswered.
Right. Why was I hesitating?
I needed to find whoever killed my friend!
I grabbed the paper firmly. He gave a satisfied smile, then in an instant his expression turned grave.
Everything we discussed today is a secret. I must insist you tell no one.
Understood.
Your friends as well. Especially—please ensure this never reaches Sonozaki-san or Furude-san.
Sonozaki—you mean Mion!? Why!? Are you saying they're connected to the incident!?
My friends' names had been dropped so suddenly, paired with an order to keep it from them—I couldn't help reacting.
...Hmm. Call it investigative necessity.
Don't dodge the question! Mion is a dear friend of mine!!
I snapped at him, but he didn't seem especially bothered.
I could explain... but it may only upset you.
Then say it!!
He hesitated a little.
His eyes drifted to the window for a moment, then he opened his mouth.
There is a possibility that the string of incidents here in Hinamizawa is the work of a village-wide conspiracy.
...Th-That's impossible!!
Don't be ridiculous!!
Where's your evidence!?
changeReason: Old translation contains extra content not present in the Japanese source.

TextENGNew: There is none. Moreover, each previous case was resolved individually, and none of the perpetrators had any direct connection to the village.
Then how could you possibly come to that conclusion!?
Every year—on the day of the Watanagashi—those whom the village considers its enemies die!! Don't you think that alone is sufficient cause for suspicion?
Only the people of Hinamizawa regarded the day of the Watanagashi as sacred. So incidents occurring only on that day implied some connection to Hinamizawa...?

First the dam construction foreman! Then a villager who supported it! Those the village held grudges against kept dying under mysterious circumstances. The means varied, but the result was always the same.
Baseless, unreasonable suspicion.
But I couldn't simply laugh it off.
Then what about the priest who died after that, and the housewife after him!? They weren't enemies of the village...!!
The priest had been expected to take a leadership role during the dam dispute, but his passivity disappointed a faction of villagers. They harbored ill will toward him.
Ill will is one thing—he didn't actually harm the village!! And the housewife? She died just because she was the wife of a supporter's brother!? That's an even thinner reason!!
And what about Tomitake-san!! He had nothing to do with the dam project at all!!! Was he targeted just for being an outsider!?
At first the victims had all been connected to the dam project—but as the years passed, the threshold of 'enemy' had been dropping steadily lower.
...That's what's frightening. The way it keeps getting lower.
What's frightening about it!?
It means they're no longer limiting themselves to enemies of the village. Being an outsider alone may now be sufficient reason.
So—next year's victim will be chosen from among 'the outsiders of the village'!?
Or perhaps someone who only recently moved here.
What exactly do you mean by...
The words died in my throat.
The biggest outsider here in Hinamizawa right now... would be... my family.
I was evidence of that myself.
I still couldn't reliably recall the names of people I passed on the street.
Then... the next victim... my family... ...are you saying it might be me...!?
But what does any of that have to do with Mion!?
"I can't go into detail, but the Sonozaki family was at the forefront of the resistance movement during the dam dispute. And in a fairly radical capacity."
changeReason: Old translates 抵抗運動 as 'dam protests' while new more accurately translates it as 'resistance movement,' implying a more organized and militant effort.

TextENGNew: Mion Sonozaki-san herself is no exception. She has a record from that period—several minor offenses and obstruction of public duties.
I knew Mion had 'fought.'
But this was the first I'd heard of her family leading the protests.
So then... what exactly did that mean...?
...Are you saying Mion's family is connected to this string of incidents?
I wouldn't go that far. I'm saying only that... if there were a conspiracy... they would be the most likely candidates.
A far from clear explanation.
I felt like he was skillfully evading everything I actually wanted to know.
We have no idea who is involved or to what extent. That is precisely why I don't want you speaking to any of the villagers.
I responded with a visible grimace instead of words. He seemed to take the message.
Then think of it this way: the villagers who devoutly believe in the curse—I don't want them to worry. Let's go with that.
'Let's go with that'—I had no idea what that was supposed to mean.
But I understood well enough that carelessly letting any of this out would be a mistake.
A perpetrator was out there somewhere.
changeReason: Old implies the perpetrator is at the current location ('was here') while JPN どこかにいる means 'is somewhere (out there)'; different spatial implication.

TextENGNew: Nothing to do with any curse.
And that person would be caught and made to answer for it—that was how this would end.
The villagers didn't need to have anything to do with that process.
People who were already on edge about the curse would only worry themselves needlessly.
...Just as everyone hid it from me to spare me the worry. Is it my turn now—to hide it from them so they don't have to worry?
I was talking to myself.
Unlike me, the others had been living with this mounting anxiety for years. What this latest incident meant to them was something else entirely.
I didn't want to burden them any further.
In the end, things played out exactly as the man had wanted—which annoyed me—but it couldn't be helped.
Understood. I'll keep it to myself. That's all right... and, your name is...?
Ooishi, from the Okinomiya Police Station. You can call me Kura-chan, if you'd like.
Ah—no, Ooishi-san is fine...
Slimy manner of speaking, yet oddly formal—exactly the kind of lecherous detective type you'd expect.
He didn't match any detective I'd ever seen in a TV drama, though.
...I've taken up too much of your time. Your friends are waiting, aren't they? You should head back.
The door opened with a click and a wave of sweltering heat rushed in.
The outside was brutal. Had today really been this hot all along...?
The cruelty of it, in contrast to the mechanical coolness of the car's interior.
It was almost as if the land of Hinamizawa had suddenly turned against me. A sad little illusion.
I wasn't telling you to suspect your friends. Please don't misunderstand.
I refrained from pointing out how convenient that was to say now.
Even if you see and hear nothing at all, Maebara-san—that itself would be meaningful. It would serve as evidence that the village is not involved.
I'll be in touch if I see or hear anything. But I'm no detective. Please don't expect too much of me.
Of course not! You don't need to play detective. Just live your life as you always have. Simply let me know if anything catches your attention.
I gave him a quick bow and hurried back toward the school building.
See you again. Farewell, Maebara-kun!
I walked back without looking around.
I wasn't sure how much time had passed.
I felt bad for making everyone wait, but I had too much weighing on my mind.
Tomitake-san's death alone was more than enough to carry... and on top of that, the possibility of a village conspiracy?
And beyond that—even Mion might be involved??
Nonsense.
Not Mion, not Rena, not Satoko, not Rika-chan. Impossible.
The people who attacked Tomitake-san were multiple?
Who on earth could they be.
And... were they hiding here, somewhere in Hinamizawa...?
I didn't know.
The only thing I could say with certainty was that Mion was not the perpetrator.
It's Mii-chan!! The perpetrator!!
I flinched at the sound of Rena's voice.
The weapon is the 'Rope,' and the crime scene is... hmm... the 'Lounge'!!!
Bwahahahaha!! Nooope~~!!
Noooooooooo!!!
Rena clutched her head and writhed.
Spectacularly self-destructed, haven't you.
Aahh!! Keiichi-san, you took forever!! I'm absolutely fuming, I'll have you know!
A-Ah, sorry about that... The teacher called me to the staff room...
You were scolded for bad behavior? ...Poor thing.
Well, it was pretty intense over here! It's getting late, so let's make this the final round.
My current score is zero, isn't it... Isn't that basically confirmed last place?
D-Don't worry, Keiichi-kun. ...Rena is at minus one. ...Hau...
changeReason: Character name 'Rena' present in new translation but absent in old.

TextENGNew: Wait, what!? Her score is lower than mine, and I wasn't even there.
This final game decides whether Rena takes the penalty alone, or Kei-chan shares it with her!!
You've got to be kidding me. Just let Rena handle the penalty herself!
...We made a promise, Keiichi-kun... that we'd both win together... I waited this whole time, you know? You know?
...Ugh... that one's on me, isn't it...
True to our original plan, I showed Rena my cards—but even when I figured out the answer, I kept quiet and simply waited for her to call it.
I've got it!!! The murderer is 'me'!! The weapon is 'Poison,' and the crime scene is the 'Entrance'!! Am I right!? Am I right!?
Oh ho, Rena—that was fast~? Don't tell me it was another wild guess... Oh.
...Rena is correct.
Oh ho ho ho!! Then Keiichi-san's penalty is confirmed as well!!
Yaaay!! Keiichi-kun, let's both give it our best!
Y-Yeah... let's do our best... hahaha...
I only had myself to blame.
And that's game! In first place—Mion Sonozaki! In dead last—Keiichi Maebara and Rena Ryuugu!!
Everyone applauded and the game was over.
...The problem was... the penalty.
Now then! Since I ranked first, I'll decide what our losers will be buying!
...Wh-What's she going to make us buy, I wonder... I wonder?
Let's agree right now—the drugstore is off-limits. That was your plan, wasn't it!!
Huh? I don't need anything from the drugstore.
What this old man wants you to buy is already written right here on this list~!
Whoa... there's a lot on here...
A-A lot!?
Damn, what is she making us buy!?
...Two blocks of tofu. Shampoo and conditioner. Mirin and fried tofu. What even is this.
It looks less like a penalty and more like... someone's shopping list...
...Mii seemed quite confident she'd win today.
Rule Seven of our club!!!! The loser may not refuse the penalty!!! Here's the money. The shampoo I always use is Fruit Story, so don't get the wrong one. Thanks!
Th-This is just your personal grocery list isn't iiiit!!!
I swore to myself that the next time I won, I was making Mion go buy hemorrhoid cream.
changeReason: Character name 'Mion' present in new translation but absent in old.
